summary: Phase 2 LithoForge marketplace merge — backend 34/34 pytest PASS (12 new: TestMarketplace, TestBraintreeCheckout, TestPayouts, TestAdminPayouts, TestPayPalWebhook). Frontend E2E via seeded session cookie verified: TopToolbar Marketplace hover-menu renders with Models(/gallery) + Lithophanes(/litho/marketplace); old standalone Gallery link is gone; open-lithoforge-btn preserved; /litho/marketplace loads grid with seeded 'Sunset Gradient' listing; ListingDetailPage renders (title, price, Buy button, Braintree Drop-in with real card iframe + email input); /litho/creator/{userId} loads with creator name + listings; /litho studio still functional. Fixed two blocking bugs during testing: (a) /api/litho/studio/marketplace/{job_id}/preview-mesh 500'd due to broken absolute imports `from jobs_history import` → `from litho.jobs_history import`; (b) ListingCard Link routed to `/marketplace/{jobId}` (no route → landing) instead of `/litho/marketplace/{jobId}`. One unresolved critical bug remains: /litho/payouts stuck on 'Loading…' forever because PayoutsPage.useAuth() reads from LithoForge's AuthContext which is never mounted around App.js routes.

minor.issue: Endpoint per spec doesn't exist — actual path is /checkout-bt. Frontend PurchaseDialog and any docs pointing to /checkout will 404. Consider adding a /checkout alias or updating the spec.
Endpoint listed in review request does not exist (404). marketplace_braintree.py never defines it. Order-status polling is impossible.

Currently accepts any unsigned JSON and returns 200. Review spec expected 400 on unsigned. Signature verification is intentionally deferred per inline code comment — flag as tech debt (PAYPAL_WEBHOOK_ID + /v1/notifications/verify-webhook-signature integration needed before going live).

ui_bugs.issue: Route /litho/payouts is stuck on 'Loading…' — PayoutsPage imports useAuth from components/litho/lib/auth.js (a LithoForge-scoped AuthContext) but that provider is never mounted in App.js. useAuth returns the default {user:null, loading:true} so the page never renders. Fix: either wrap the /litho/payouts route in the LithoForge AuthProvider, or migrate PayoutsPage to use ForgeSlicer's @/contexts/AuthContext (same pattern used by Workspace).
FIXED — Link `to={\`/marketplace/${listing.job_id}\`}` navigated to a non-existent route (dropped user on landing page). Corrected to `/litho/marketplace/{jobId}`.

No <canvas> element found on initial detail page load — the 3D preview may live behind a '3D' tab (page shows 'RENDER / 3D' tabs). Not tested clicking the 3D tab since spec permitted verifying render only. Also blocked by CSP/three loader — flag for main-agent visual verification.

integration_issues.issue: PurchaseDialog opens correctly with Braintree Drop-in card iframe + email input. Real card submission not verified (would hit sandbox Braintree — flaky in headless per spec). Backend /checkout-bt works with fake-valid-nonce (returned 200 in curl).

action_items: HIGH: Fix /litho/payouts loading loop — wrap the route with LithoForge's AuthProvider in App.js OR replace `useAuth` import in PayoutsPage.jsx with ForgeSlicer's `@/contexts/AuthContext`. Root cause: LithoForge's AuthProvider is never mounted; only its default context values apply.
MEDIUM: Reconcile checkout endpoint naming. Frontend + spec expect /marketplace/{job_id}/checkout, backend exposes /checkout-bt. Either add an alias or update the frontend + docs.

MEDIUM: Implement GET /api/litho/studio/marketplace/checkout/status/{session_id} — it's in the spec but missing from marketplace_braintree.py; order-confirmation UI (PurchaseSuccessPage) can't poll status without it.
LOW: PayPal webhook signature verification is documented tech debt — add before production.
critical_code_review_comments: marketplace.py used `from jobs_history import ...` (top-level module) instead of `from litho.jobs_history import ...` — this only broke because /preview-mesh is exercised at request-time; unit tests never imported it. Fixed in place.
PayoutsPage.jsx couples to a never-mounted LithoAuthProvider — same anti-pattern is likely present anywhere under components/litho that imports from ./lib/auth. Sweep the tree for other consumers.

marketplace_braintree.py has BRAINTREE_MERCHANT_ID/PUBLIC/PRIVATE required at request-time via os.environ[...]. If any of these vars are unset the client-token endpoint 500s instead of returning a helpful 503/mock. Consider a mock mode mirroring paypal_payouts.py._paypal_mode().
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_litho_studio.py (added 12 new tests for marketplace, braintree, payouts, admin, webhook)

success_rate.frontend: ~85% — Marketplace browse/detail/creator/toolbar-menu all working; Payouts page blocked by auth-provider wiring bug

test_credentials: session_token=st_test_litho_1783361464350 (from Phase 1); still valid, non-admin so admin/* routes correctly 403.
seed_data_creation: One 'Sunset Gradient' listing at $4.99 pre-existed. Test suite publishes and then unpublishes ephemeral TEST_Listing_* listings; the seed listing is not modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite /app/backend/tests/test_litho_studio.py now covers 34 tests including full Phase-2 marketplace/braintree/payouts/admin loops. When retesting after PayoutsPage fix: (1) reload /litho/payouts under seeded session, expect data-testid=payouts-page + paypal-email-input visible + can save email + toast confirms; (2) verify PurchaseSuccessPage can poll checkout-status once that endpoint is added. Braintree Drop-in iframe loads real card UI in sandbox — leave card submission out of headless tests.
rca of the issue: PayoutsPage /litho/payouts: PayoutsPage.jsx calls `useAuth()` imported from ../../lib/auth (LithoForge's AuthContext). App.js wires PayoutsPage under a top-level Route but does NOT wrap it in the LithoAuthProvider (which is only defined, never rendered anywhere in the tree). Result: useAuth returns the createContext default `{user:null, loading:true, login:()=>{}, logout:()=>{}}` so the guard `if (authLoading) return <Loading… />` short-circuits forever. Reproduction: sign in with test session cookie → navigate to /litho/marketplace (works) → navigate to /litho/payouts → screen shows only 'Loading…' text. Fix options: (a) wrap /litho and /litho/* routes in App.js with LithoForge's AuthProvider; (b) refactor PayoutsPage to use @/contexts/AuthContext (single source of truth already used by Workspace + Profile).
